channel.title: Opinion on The Daily Thrum
channel.description: Recent content in Opinion on The Daily Thrum

item.title: I Don’t Post About Candidate Experience On LinkedIn Because I’m Not A Pussy
item.description:
By Ryan Hollis

There is a certain kind of LinkedIn post that appears every few weeks, usually from someone who has just completed seven rounds of interviews, performed a live coding exercise, submitted a blood sample, explained how their personal values align with “customer obsession”, and then been left staring into the void by a recruiter who has discovered the ancient corporate art of saying nothing.

The post usually begins with “Candidate experience matters”.

item.title: Building Data Centres In Other People’s Suburbs Is A Price I’m Willing To Pay
item.description:
I have thought deeply about Australia’s growing data centre debate, and after careful consideration I have reached a principled position.

Build them.

Build hundreds.

Build them wherever necessary.

Just not near me.

Some people say data centres consume too much electricity, strain the grid, require enormous water and cooling infrastructure, generate noise, increase demand for transmission upgrades, and turn otherwise liveable areas into humming concrete shrines to server load.

To those people I say: yes, probably.
